title: Heavy rains and typhoons hit several regions of Japan with maximum alerts
summary: Intense rainfall causes evacuations, suspension of trains, and closure of factories, as two typhoons approach the archipelago.

title: Strong earthquake shakes Aomori prefecture and leaves at least 11 injured
summary: The magnitude 6.2 earthquake caused damage to homes and raised concern among residents about possible aftershocks.

title: School bus invades opposite lane and causes multiple collision in Osaka
summary: The accident involved six vehicles and left between 5 and 12 injured; the driver was arrested for dangerous driving.

title: Japanese government presents a bill to reform the Imperial Household Law
summary: The initiative seeks to ensure the continuity of the imperial family through the adoption of male members, but faces criticism in Parliament.

title: Leak of personal data of judicial reviewers in Yamaguchi prosecutor's office
summary: The Yamaguchi District Public Prosecutors Office described the disclosure of the names of eleven reviewers as 'extremely inappropriate'.

title: Japan faces Sweden in the World Cup with chances to advance to the round of 16
summary: The Japanese national team seeks victory against Sweden to secure its passage to the next round of the tournament.

title: Two earthquakes of magnitude over 7 cause more than 160 deaths in Venezuela
summary: The country declared a state of emergency after the earthquakes hit aging infrastructure, with an estimate of up to 10,000 victims.

title: Yuko Obuchi resigns from the vice presidency of the LDP tax committee
summary: The former minister opposed the proposal to reduce the consumption tax to 1%, which led to her departure from the position.

title: Nikkei and Dow Jones hit new historic records driven by artificial intelligence
summary: The Nikkei index rose more than 3,000 points and the Dow Jones surpassed $700, renewing their all-time highs.

title: Japanese government delays goal of 1,500 yen minimum wage
summary: The goal is postponed until the early 2030s, according to the prime minister's announcement amid criticism from the business sector.

title: Takaichi government proposes budget reform with a new investment framework
summary: The reform includes the creation of an 'investment framework' without spending limits, which generates controversy in Parliament.

title: Yen weakens to 161.8 per dollar, nearing lows not seen in decades
summary: The Japanese currency continues its downward trend amid expectations that the Fed will maintain a restrictive monetary policy.

title: Japan's Lower House passes bill to regulate social media in election campaigns
summary: The initiative seeks to reduce online misinformation, in a context marked by the scandal of the defamatory video against the prime minister.

title: Hong Kong police arrest bookstore owners for inciting hatred against the government
summary: The detainees are accused of selling publications that foster antagonism towards the Chinese authorities.

title: Disappearances of people with dementia in Japan decrease for second consecutive year
summary: In 2023, 17,345 cases were reported, but the use of GPS devices and anti-theft tags helped locate 139 people.

title: Professional baseball day in Japan with notable victories
summary: Chunichi Dragons beat DeNA, Maeda Kenta achieved his first win after returning to NPB, and Suzuki Gota debuted with a victory.

title: US Senate rejects Iran withdrawal bill and nuclear negotiations will resume
summary: The vote took an unexpected turn when a Republican senator changed his vote, while new technical talks are expected on the 30th.

title: Pakistani man sentenced to 23 years in prison acquitted due to translation errors in trial
summary: The appeals court determined that the interpreter exceeded the limits of literal translation, which led to the quashing of the conviction.

title: The Emperor and Empress of Japan conclude their official visit to Belgium
summary: The imperial couple bid farewell to the Belgian king and queen and began their return to Tokyo after completing the official agenda.

title: Amazon announces investment of 2.1 trillion yen in India
summary: Amazon's CEO met with Prime Minister Modi to announce the investment plan, which seeks to expand its operations in the country.
